title: A strengthened and fit-for purpose State Hydrometorological Service in Moldova: a road map
content: This Roadmap presents an analysis of the current capabilities, including financial and human capital, and technical infrastructure of the State Hydrometeorological Services (SHS) of Moldova

title: A spatiotemporal-magnitude integrated framework for debris flow activity assessment in the Eastern Himalayan syntaxis
content: This study addresses a gap in regional-scale debris flow research, which has traditionally focused on susceptibility mapping without adequately capturing frequency and magnitude — key elements needed for effective disaster mitigation.

title: After action review: Facilitation guide
content: The Anticipation Hub has published the first edition of its facilitation guide for collective, multi-agency After Action Reviews. These events help partners to learn from how they planned for, coordinated ahead of and acted during a forecast hazard.

title: Indigenous peoples and knowledge in climate adaptation planning for mountain regions
content: This brief examines how Indigenous Peoples and Indigenous knowledge are reflected in National Adaptation Plans across mountain regions worldwide.
snippet: This brief examines how Indigenous Peoples and Indigenous knowledge are reflected in National Adaptation Plans across mountain regions worldwide.

title: The role of social assistance in advancing climate adaptation and nature based solutions in least developed countries
content: This working paper examines how social assistance programs can support climate adaptation and nature-based solutions (NbS) in least developed countries (LDCs).
snippet: This working paper examines how social assistance programs can support climate adaptation and nature-based solutions (NbS) in least developed countries (LDCs).

title: People are getting financial incentives to use less electricity during heat waves

title: Fossil fuels are heating America’s 250th birthday
content: A widespread heat wave is forecast to affect much of the central and eastern United States over the Fourth of July weekend, driven by a strong “heat dome”, a high pressure system sitting over the US, bringing moisture and warm air from the Gulf of Mexico.

title: As Europe’s heat wave shifts east, seasonal resilience is no longer enough
content: Last week, Europe received another stark reminder that extreme heat is no longer an exceptional event.
snippet: Last week, Europe received another stark reminder that extreme heat is no longer an exceptional event.

title: In Guatemala, Indigenous women build climate resilience with old and new farming methods

snippet: Ancestral knowledge combined with climate-smart techniques is boosting food security and increasing family incomes in the Lake Atitlán region.

title: Post-disaster recovery in Sint Maarten
content: This study examined recovery needs and actions after Hurricanes Irma and Maria in Sint Maarten (2017), using a SIDS recovery framework to assess whether key sectors and recovery priorities were adequately addressed.

title: Mapping Africa’s changing coastline
content: A new interactive platform mapping sea-level change across Africa reveals how coastal impacts vary dramatically from place to place — reshaping how scientists and policymakers understand risk along some of the continent’s most vulnerable shorelines.

title: Nepal’s improving but inadequate monsoon disaster preparedness
content: True preparedness means resilient infrastructure, investment in disaster risk reduction and stabilising fragile slopes.
snippet: True preparedness means resilient infrastructure, investment in disaster risk reduction and stabilising fragile slopes.

title: China launches oceanographic satellite for enhanced marine monitoring
content: China launched a Long March 4B carrier rocket to deploy its latest oceanographic research satellite, the HY-2E, on Thursday morning, according to the China National Space Administration.
snippet: China launched a Long March 4B carrier rocket to deploy its latest oceanographic research satellite, the HY-2E, on Thursday morning, according to the China National Space Administration.

title: Communication and media presentation specialist (National)

snippet: ADPC intends to hire a National Consultant on a output-based lump-sum contract related to communication and media presentation for modernizing DOM’s services and functions.

title: Consultant for concept design and technical advisory on specialized SAR training facilities

title: Climate risks to Syria’s electricity systems
content: This briefing paper presents key findings from a semi-quantitative climate risk assessment of Syria's electricity sector, intended to support the country's reconstruction efforts.
snippet: This briefing paper presents key findings from a semi-quantitative climate risk assessment of Syria's electricity sector, intended to support the country's reconstruction efforts.

title: Race to beat the heat: climate change impacts physical activity
content: This article examines the impact of climate change—particularly the increasing frequency, intensity, and duration of extreme heat events—on physical activity and sports participation.
snippet: This article examines the impact of climate change—particularly the increasing frequency, intensity, and duration of extreme heat events—on physical activity and sports participation.

title: Overcoming the limitations of social media analytics: Dynamic capabilities and workaround strategies for inclusive and resilient public service continuity

title: Diverse connections between climate extremes and agricultural loss across the Southern Slopes of the Himalayas

snippet: This report assesses how drought, heat stress, and extreme precipitation influence crop productivity under single and double cropping systems at the Southern Slopes of the Himalaya.

title: Avalanche risk governance in Türkiye (2000–2025): institutional fragmentation, international models, and policy options

snippet: This article conducts a structured institutional and policy analysis, combining a focused review of Türkiye’s disaster governance architecture and legal framework.

title: Climate change will raise the risk of severe heatwaves. NZ homes aren’t ready

title: El Niño is forecast to intensify, increasing likelihood of extreme weather
content: El Niño conditions have developed in the tropical Pacific and are forecast to strengthen rapidly over the coming months, increasing the likelihood of heatwaves, droughts, heavy rainfall and other extreme weather events in many parts of the world.

title: Sea turtles diving through the eye of the storm help develop better cyclone forecasts
content: Every summer, communities across northern Australia brace for the tropical cyclone season. Tropical cyclones draw their power from the warm seas, extracting heat and moisture from ocean water.
snippet: Every summer, communities across northern Australia brace for the tropical cyclone season. Tropical cyclones draw their power from the warm seas, extracting heat and moisture from ocean water.

title: The world’s oceans are the hottest on record for June – and El Niño is set to turn up the heat even more
content: The world’s oceans are the hottest on record for June, pushing past records set during the 2023–24 El Niño years.
